checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and flow of the source PDF. Content is presented in the same order, starting from the cover page elements, followed by a functional table of contents, and then the body sections from the Introduction through to the Appendices. The transition from a paginated PDF to a continuous HTML document does not disrupt the meaning or hierarchy of the information.

All tables present in the source PDF (Workshops, Study Group, Population breakdowns, Call Volume, Goal objectives, and Appendix data) are rendered correctly in the HTML. They feature clear column headers, aligned row data, and visible borders that allow for easy reading and understanding. The complex financial spreadsheets from pages 31-32 of the PDF are rendered as images in the HTML, which is an acceptable method to preserve their visual integrity and alignment for highly detailed tabular data.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the North Carolina flag and the Ritter Strategic Services logo on the cover page, as well as the 'Simplified NENA i3 NG911 Conceptual System Diagram' (Figure 1) in section 3.2. These images are properly rendered and placed within their respective sections.

There is no evidence of body content duplication in the HTML output. Each paragraph, table, and image appears exactly once in the document flow, mirroring the structure of the source PDF. While headings appear in both the navigation/TOC area and the body, this is an intentional accessibility feature and not a duplication of content.
